Home › Districts › Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf
West · Inner city 9 editor's picks

Hotels in Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

Charlottenburg is Berlin's elegant West — Kurfürstendamm, Charlottenburg Palace, KaDeWe and Savignyplatz define the district. This is where you find Berlin's most traditional 4- and 5-star hotels in late-19th-century buildings and quiet side streets.

Kurfürstendamm Charlottenburg Palace KaDeWe Savignyplatz Memorial Church Olympic Stadium
How the district feels

Atmosphere

Elegant, refined, calm. Shopping on Ku'damm, coffee at Savignyplatz, classical concerts at the Philharmonie.

Ideal for

Who stays here right

  • ✓ Discerning travellers
  • ✓ Shopping fans
  • ✓ Classical music lovers
  • ✓ Business travellers

Strong presence

Popular hotel styles in Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

✦

Luxury Hotels

5-star, concierge service, spa, Michelin restaurant. Especially in Mitte and Charlottenburg.

from €280/night hotels
◐

Charming Hotels

Personally run, with history, often owner-managed. For travellers who want character.

from €120/night hotels
▣

Business Hotels

Conference rooms, fast Wi-Fi, breakfast from 6am, good transport links.

from €130/night hotels
→

Hotels with Shuttle

Transfers to/from BER, trade fair or stations — ideal for late-night arrivals.

from €100/night hotels
▢

Apartment Hotels

With kitchen and living area — ideal for longer stays and families.

from €95/night hotels
Nearby

Attractions in Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

The key landmarks, museums and parks easily reached from your hotel in Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf.

Marek Śliwecki · CC BY-SA 4.0
Shopping · Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

Kurfürstendamm

The most famous shopping street in the west — 3.5 km boulevard with KaDeWe, boutiques, cafés and the Memorial Church at its end.

ernstol · CC BY-SA 3.0
Landmark · Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

Charlottenburg Palace

Berlin's largest palace — baroque splendour with palace gardens, Belvedere and mausoleum. Ideal for a half-day visit.

Nikolai Schwerg · CC BY-SA 3.0
Landmark · Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

Olympic Stadium

Venue of the 1936 Olympic Games, today home of Hertha BSC and stage for major concerts. Impressive architecture.

Jörg Zägel · CC BY-SA 3.0
Shopping · Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf

KaDeWe

Continental Europe's largest department store — seven floors, legendary Gourmet food hall. A Berlin institution.

Keep exploring

Other Berlin districts

All 12 districts
H.Helmlechner · CC BY-SA 4.0
Centre 11 hotels

Mitte

Stately, history-rich, urban-elegant. Tourists by day, theatre and fine dining by night.

Brandenburg Gate Museum Island Reichstag
Dietmar Rabich · CC BY-SA 4.0
East 4 hotels

Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg

Alternative, creative, vibrant. Nightlife on the edge, street art on every corner, cafés open late.

East Side Gallery Mercedes-Benz Arena RAW area
nikey · CC BY-SA 4.0
North 4 hotels

Pankow

Family-friendly, green, relaxed. Cafés with kids' corners, Sundays at Mauerpark, stuccoed period buildings.

Mauerpark Kollwitzplatz Kulturbrauerei
Reinhold Möller Ermell · CC BY-SA 4.0
South 2 hotels

Neukölln

Hip, diverse, creative, young. Brunch spots, late-night kiosks, bargain markets, gallery openings.

Tempelhofer Feld Maybachufer Klunkerkranich
